markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose backend APIs for claiming worktrees/workspaces, listing active claims, and releasing or reclaiming stale claims. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: WorktreeWorkspaceClaimApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: WorktreeWorkspaceClaimApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: Parallel swarm state recovery hardening for MT-209..216

evidence.summary: MT-209..216 implement lane/capability model, claim/lease receipts, mailbox/handoff/checkpoint/recovery atomicity, recovery/reclaim behavior, attribution scrub, and EventLedger-backed state recovery surfaces.

commands.result: PASS; 16/16 parallel swarm state recovery tests passed
PASS; test target registered without duplicate names

evidence.summary: Reviewer passed MT-209..216 after capability, lease, mailbox, receipt, handoff/checkpoint/recovery, and claim/release atomicity hardening.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-210 requires backend APIs to claim worktrees/workspaces, list active claims, and release/reclaim stale claims with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code delivers all four operations as real, atomic, EventLedger-backed PostgreSQL functions, proven at runtime against a Handshake-managed PostgreSQL cluster with isolated schemas and claim/release/reclaim receipts asserted via direct SQL.
findings: claim_work_surface (state_recovery.rs:1340) implements the claim API: validates TTL+scope+capability, opportunistically reclaims expired claims, returns Held when an active holder exists, otherwise inserts an 'active' row in knowledge_agent_worktree_claims in a transaction and writes a SessionClaimed EventLedger receipt; unique-violation race returns the existing holder (state_recovery.rs:1438).
list_active_claims (state_recovery.rs:1455) reclaims expired claims first, then selects active, non-released, non-expired claims for the workspace - correct canonical-state read.

release_claim (state_recovery.rs:2329) uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE scoped to actor_id+active, writes a release receipt, sets status='released' with release_event_ledger_event_id, and returns false if caller is not the active holder.

reclaim_expired_work_claims (state_recovery.rs:3551) gates on ClaimWorktree capability, locks expired active claims FOR UPDATE, writes a SessionCancelled reclaim receipt per claim, and updates status='reclaimed' RETURNING the row, all in one transaction.

Migration 0311_parallel_swarm_state_recovery.sql:6-36 creates knowledge_agent_worktree_claims with FK refs to kernel_event_ledger, a status CHECK constraint, and a partial UNIQUE index ux_agent_worktree_claims_active_scope enforcing one active claim per (scope_kind, scope_id) - the real exclusivity primitive. Registered in knowledge_schema_registry under mt_id MT-210 (line 149-150).

Runtime proof is real PG: recovery_store() (tests:174) connects via knowledge_pg() which uses ManagedPostgres::ensure_running (knowledge_pg_support.rs:49-64) - the Handshake-managed PostgreSQL cluster with per-test isolated schemas and real migrations. No SQLite, no mock store.

Test agent_lanes_and_work_claims_are_typed_attributable_and_exclusive (tests:776) proves the full MT-210 cycle: claim Active -> second claim Held naming holder -> list_active_claims len==1 -> release -> SQL assertion that release_event_ledger_event_id starts with 'KE-' -> re-claim Active. explicit_expired_claim_reclaim_records_event_receipt (tests:5642) forces expiry in real PG and asserts reclaim status plus a kernel_event_ledger reclaim receipt counted by SQL. Atomicity failure-injection tests exist (release/reclaim rollback when receipt insert fails, tests:940/5709).

sub_rule_1: grep of state_recovery.rs and the test file found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/*Unavailable/'pending MT-'/'deferred to follow-on' on any live claim path. The None SKIP path (no PG binaries) is a host-availability guard, not a stand-in for required behavior.

Spec alignment: Master Spec v02.194 module 05-security-and-observability.md:1860 (reclaim hooks on session staleness) and 06-mechanical-integrations.md:6796 (parallel governed sessions contending for leases, concurrency invariants) confirm parallel-agent claim/lease/reclaim is in-scope native runtime behavior; the impl matches.

Aside (non-blocking, governance only): lifecycle.claimed_by is null while completed_by is KERNEL_BUILDER, so this is not a clean two-actor handoff; per ground rules self-cert alone is not a fail and the proof here is real PG runtime evidence.
evidence_checked: MT-210.json (contract: scope, acceptance_criteria, constraints, red_team, lifecycle.implementation_commit e6aea0fc)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
